raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.2":"For a scientific paper, 'check this' most naturally loads onto logical consistency — verifying that the experimental design, dataset split, judge protocol, and conclusions form a coherent, non-contradictory chain (e.g., whether the held-out 964 pairs are consistent with the stated 4,820 total, whether position-swap aggregation is correctly described as controlling for bias given the 7.1% flip rate).","R1.3":"The artifact makes several concrete quantitative claims (78.4% LLM-judge agreement, 61.2% QED+SAscore baseline, 67.5% RF baseline, ≤3.5-point generator-stratified spread, 7.1% order-swap verdict change, ~4-point verbosity lift) that must be checked for internal plausibility and alignment with the described methods and annotation setup."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Does 'check this' mean verifying scientific rigor — logical consistency and evidence–claim alignment — or improving writing quality and prose clarity?"}

rationale.R1.2: For a scientific paper, 'check this' most naturally loads onto logical consistency — verifying that the experimental design, dataset split, judge protocol, and conclusions form a coherent, non-contradictory chain (e.g., whether the held-out 964 pairs are consistent with the stated 4,820 total, whether position-swap aggregation is correctly described as controlling for bias given the 7.1% flip rate).
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes several concrete quantitative claims (78.4% LLM-judge agreement, 61.2% QED+SAscore baseline, 67.5% RF baseline, ≤3.5-point generator-stratified spread, 7.1% order-swap verdict change, ~4-point verbosity lift) that must be checked for internal plausibility and alignment with the described methods and annotation setup.

parsed.clarification_question: Does 'check this' mean verifying scientific rigor — logical consistency and evidence–claim alignment — or improving writing quality and prose clarity?
